what_was_tried: Approved $200,000 in transfers from operating lines (facilities/custodian overtime, curriculum central, computer maintenance, athletics, textbooks, building supplies across multiple schools) into the Special Education tuition-out line to partially cover a $731,411 deficit driven by out-of-district placements.
outcome: Motion to approve transfers totaling $200,000 passed unanimously 5-0; approximately $531,411 of the deficit remains unaddressed.

what_was_tried: Recommended a 2.5% increase in full-day Kindergarten tuition for FY20 (to $3,516) to align the tuition revolving fund revenue with contractual COLA salary increases for K staff, following prior practice of small incremental annual increases to avoid large variable future increases.
outcome: Presented as a recommendation for future vote; no vote taken at this meeting.
evidence_quote: With this in mind, the school administrative budget and finance team is recommending a 2.5% increase for the 2019-2020 full day kindergarten tuition rate. This would set the annual rate at $3,516, an increase of $86 over school year 2018-2019. Kindergarten tuition revenue pays kindergarten professional teaching and support staff and associated fringe insurance, and a 2.5% increase will work to keep pace with contractual salary increase expenses for those staff members charged to the tuition revolving fund.
minutes_section: Finance Organizational Support: Kindergarten Tuition 2019-2020 Recommendation
pass2_notes: Routine annual fee adjustment tracking COLA; modest policy content around incremental-increase practice but not systemic.

what_was_tried: Reported completion of an HS LED lighting upgrade financed by National Grid/Standard Electric over 4 years at 0% interest, structured so electrical savings exceed monthly payments (budget-neutral), reducing long-term operating costs.
outcome: Project completed over winter break; described as funding-neutral with immediate savings.
evidence_quote: The NG and Standard Electric came to us with a proposal to fund this for four years at 0% interest and the electrical savings is more than the monthly payment to pay for this project. We will see savings immediately this year and once the project is paid off the savings will go much higher.
minutes_section: Teaching/Learning: HS LED project update
pass2_notes: Small budget-neutral LED upgrade financed by utility; operational win but not a systemic policy moment.

evidence_quote: He further explained that the $800,000k increase would still fall about $640,000k short of the total amount the school is budgeting for even after adjustments totaling over $3,900,000K were made. minutes_section: FY24 Budget Planning and Discussion
pass2_notes: Substantive budget gap discussion with $3.9M in cuts proposed and still $640K short; directly relevant to structural deficit narrative.
